violations.text: The following areas of the floors/walls/ceiling were observed in need of repair/adjustment... - Floor tiles were observed to be cracked and or missing exposing the unsealed subfloor - The painted ceiling was observed to have some peeling - Gaps were observed in the wall and ceiling coverings at seams where the walls meet the ceiling The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair (work already observed to have begun since previous inspection). New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The menu was observed to be lacking an allergen awareness statement. An allergen awareness statement is to be provided for customer viewing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Home Fry Potatoes, which was prepared in advance previous day, were not cooled to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within the total of 6 hours. Proper cooling time and temperature was discussed with the PIC at time of inspection. Potatoes were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Several food items held in the top portion of the cookline prep units were observed held at 42 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Based on time of food items placed in prep units from walk-in, food items were moved back to the walk-in for rapid cooling and replaced with colder product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Pans of Home Fry Potatoes prepped earlier same day were observed cooling in pans placed directly on top of each other. Proper cooling methods were reviewed, potatoes were repositioned for rapid cooling at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

(ROP) Reduced Oxygen Packaged Lobster was observed stored thawed in the walk-in cooler in its intact packaging at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Proper thawing of Reduced Oxygen Seafood was discussed with the PIC at the time of inspection. ROP Lobster was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

The screen door(s) to the outside, located in the kitchen area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2025
The hand sink at the end of the cookline was observed to be loosely fitting into the counter and in need of adjustment/repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2025
The sink used for food preparation is lacking an adequate air gap. An adequate space/separation between the sink drain and the plumbing below is to be provided. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2025

The following areas of the floors/walls/ceiling were observed in need of repair/adjustment... - Floor tiles were observed to be cracked and or missing exposing the unsealed subfloor - The painted ceiling was observed to have some peeling - The wall behind the dish machine was observed to be missing a cap/top plate exposing the inner structure - Gaps were observed in the wall and ceiling coverings at seams where the walls meet the ceiling The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2025
